another 1am and i'm still refreshing the deploy dashboard. Deborah pinged me at 11:47pm, "quick question", which is never quick and never a question, it's always another rock on the pile. the Nexatech migration was supposed to take six weeks and we're in month four and every standup i say "on track" while my eye twitches. i moved to Austin for this job. i keep doing the math on my calendar: 61 hours last week, 58 the week before. my girlfriend has started saving me plates in the fridge with sticky notes that say "eat me, coward" and honestly the sticky notes are the best part of my day, which is its own kind of sad. i know what i would tell a friend in my position. i would tell him the company will not remember the weekends you gave it. i would tell him no one on their deathbed wishes they'd closed more tickets. but it's 1am and i'm refreshing the dashboard again because if the deploy fails it's my name on the postmortem. i can feel myself becoming someone i don't want to introduce to people. tomorrow i'm going to say the sentence out loud in the planning meeting: the timeline is fiction. tomorrow. probably.
